data.content:
The user wants to know about this repository. I should explore the repository structure to understand what it contains. Let me start by looking at the directory structure, README files, and key files like package.json, Cargo.toml, or other configuration files that might indicate what this project is about.

I'll start by:
1. Looking at the root directory structure
2. Finding and reading any README files
3. Looking at configuration files to understand the tech stack
4. Getting a general overview of the codebase

result.result:
{"result":" 1 # вҡ—пёҸ Crucible 2 3 [![CI](https://github.com/Mootikins/crucible/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/Mootikins/crucible/actions/workflows/ci.yml) 4 [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT%2FApache--2.0-blue.svg)](LICENSE-MIT) 5 [![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-mootikins.github.io%2Fcrucible-blue)](https://mootikins.github.io/crucible/) 6 7 **A knowledge-grounded agent runtime. Agents that draw from a knowledge graph make better decisions.** 8 9 Local-first. No cloud. No lock-in. Your conversations, notes, and wikilinks form a knowledge graph that agents draw from and contribute to вҖ” all as markdown files you own. 10 11

12 13

14 15 > **Early Development**: APIs and storage formats may change. Contributions welcome! 16 17 ## What Makes Crucible Different 18 19 Memory and knowledge are too fundamental to be an afterthought. Most AI tools treat conversations as disposable вҖ” Crucible makes them the foundation. 20 21 - **Knowledge-grounded agents.** Precognition auto-injects relevant context from your knowledge graph before each LLM turn. Block-level embeddings power semantic search at paragraph granularity. The more you use it, the smarter your agents get. 22 - **Sessions are notes.** Every chat saves as markdown in your kiln. Search them, link them with wikilinks, version them in git. Conversations become permanent, connectable knowledge. 23 - **Neovim-like architecture.** Lua/Fennel plugins, TUI-first, headless daemon with RPC. Most behaviors beyond the knowledge core can be scripted. 24 - **Bring any LLM.** Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, local GGUF models. Swap freely. 25 - **Plaintext first.** No proprietary formats. Files are the source of truth. The database is optional acceleration. 26 27 ## How It Compares 28 29 | | Crucible | ChatGPT | Obsidian + AI | OpenClaw | 30 |---|---|---|---|---| 31 | Local-first | вң… | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | 32 | Sessions as markdown | вң… | вқҢ | вқҢ | вқҢ | 33 | Knowledge graph | вң… | вқҢ | вң… | вқҢ | 34 | Bring your own LLM | вң… | вқҢ | Partial | вң… | 35 | Plugin system | вң… Lua/Fennel | вқҢ | вң… JS | вң… TS | 36 | MCP server | вң… | вқҢ | вқҢ | вқҢ | 37 | Semantic search | вң… Block-level | вқҢ | Plugin | вқҢ | 38 | Setup time | ~2 min | 0 | ~5 min | 2-7 hrs | 39 40 ## Install 41 42 **Pre-built binaries** (Linux x86_64/aarch64, macOS Apple Silicon): 43 44 ```bash 45 curl -fsSL https://github.com/Mootikins/crucible/releases/latest/download/crucible-cli-installer.sh | sh 46 ``` 47 48 **From source:** 49 50 ```bash 51 cargo install --git https://github.com/Mootikins/crucible.git crucible-cli 52 ``` 53 54 ## Quick Start 55 56 ```bash 57 # Start a chat session 58 cru chat 59 60 # Chat with Claude Code, enriched by your knowledge base 61 cru chat -a claude 62 63 # Or start the MCP server for Claude/GPT integration 64 cru mcp 65 ``` 66 67 First run prompts for a kiln path and detects available LLM providers. A background daemon auto-spawns via `cru daemon serve` to manage session state, file watching, and multi-session support. It communicates over a Unix socket and restarts automatically if stopped. 68 69 **In a chat session:** 70 - Type naturally, the agent responds with access to your knowledge base 71 - `/search query` injects relevant notes into context 72 - `:model`, `:set`, `:export` for REPL commands 73 - `BackTab` cycles modes: Normal вҶ’ Plan вҶ’ Auto 74 75

76 77

78 79 ## Features 80 81 ### Agent Chat 82 83 Interactive conversations with full session persistence. The TUI supports streaming markdown, tool calls, and multi-turn context. Sessions save as markdown files organized by workspace. 84 85 ### Knowledge Graph 86 87 Wikilinks (`[[Note Name]]`) define your graph. No extraction step, no special syntax beyond what you'd write naturally. Query by graph traversal, semantic similarity, tags, or full-text search. 88 89 ### MCP Server 90 91 Expose your knowledge base to any MCP-compatible AI (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, GPT, local models): 92 93 ```bash 94 cru mcp 95 ``` 96 97 Tools include `semantic_search`, `create_note`, `get_outlinks`, `get_inlinks`, and more. 98 99 ### Agent Integration (ACP) 100 101 Crucible can spawn and orchestrate external AI agents through the [Agent Context Protocol](https://agentcontextprotocol.org/). Your agent gets full access to Crucible's knowledge graph, semantic search, and tools. 102 103 ```bash 104 # Use Claude Code with your knowledge base 105 cru chat -a claude 106 107 # Use OpenCode 108 cru chat -a opencode 109 110 # Use Gemini CLI 111 cru chat -a gemini 112 ``` 113 114 Built-in agents (auto-discovered if installed): 115 116 | Agent | Command | Install | 117 |-------|---------|---------| 118 | opencode | `opencode` | `go install github.com/grafana/opencode@latest` | 119 | claude | `npx @zed-industries/claude-agent-acp` | `npm install -g @zed-industries/claude-agent-acp` | 120 | gemini | `gemini` | `npm install -g gemini-cli` | 121 | codex | `npx @zed-industries/codex-acp` | `npm install -g @zed-industries/codex-acp` | 122 | cursor | `cursor-acp` | `npm install -g cursor-acp` | 123 124 Agents can delegate tasks to each other. An ACP agent like Claude can hand off work to Cursor or OpenCode mid-conversation using the `delegate_session` tool, then incorporate the results. Delegation works both directions: internal agents can delegate to ACP agents, and ACP agents can delegate to other ACP agents. 125 126 Custom profiles go in `crucible.toml`: 127 128 ```toml 129 [acp.agents.my-claude] 130 extends = "claude" 131 env = { ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL = "http://localhost:4000" } 132 ``` 133 134 Then: `cru chat -a my-claude` 135 136 ### Lua Plugins 137 138 Drop `.lua` or `.fnl` files into `~/.config/crucible/plugins/` or your kiln's `plugins/` directory: 139 140 ```lua 141 -- @tool name="summarize" description="Summarize notes matching query" 142 -- @param query string "Search query" 143 function summarize(args) 144 local results = crucible.search(args.query) 145 return { summary = "Found " .. #results .. " notes" } 146 end 147 ``` 148 149 See the [plugin guide](./docs/Help/Concepts/Scripting%20Languages.md) for the full API. 150 151 ## Documentation 152 153 - **[Documentation Site](https://mootikins.github.io/crucible/)** вҖ” searchable, organized reference 154 - **[docs/](./docs/)** is both the user guide and a working example kiln (155 interlinked notes) 155 - **[AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md)** covers architecture and AI agent instructions 156 157 ## Command Reference 158 159 | Command | Alias | Description | 160 |---------|-------|-------------| 161 | `cru chat` | `c` | Interactive AI chat with session persistence | 162 | `cru chat -a ` | | Use an ACP agent (claude, opencode, gemini, etc.) | 163 | `cru chat --resume ` | | Resume a previous session | 164 | `cru mcp` | | Start MCP server for external AI agents | 165 | `cru process` | `p` | Parse, enrich, and store markdown files | 166 | `cru init` | `i` | Initialize a new kiln (workspace) | 167 | `cru session create` | | Create a new session (add `--agent ` for ACP) | 168 | `cru session list` | `s` | List sessions (live by default, `--all` includes persisted) | 169 | `cru session show ` | | Show session details (daemon first, file fallback) | 170 | `cru session open ` | | Open a previous session in the TUI | 171 | `cru session send "msg"` | | Send a message and stream the response | 172 | `cru session configure ` | | Set agent backend (provider, model, endpoint) | 173 | `cru session pause ` | | Pause a running daemon session | 174 | `cru session resume ` | | Resume a paused daemon session | 175 | `cru session end ` | | End a daemon session | 176 | `cru session export ` | | Export session to markdown | 177 | `cru session search ` | | Search sessions by title | 178 | `cru set key=val` | | Tweak runtime settings (model, temperature, etc.) | 179 | `cru stats` | | Display kiln statistics | 180 | `cru status` | | Storage status and metrics | 181 | `cru models` | | List available LLM models | 182 | `cru config init` | `cfg` | Initialize config file | 183 | `cru config show` | | Show effective configuration | 184 | `cru agents list` | | List registered agent cards | 185 | `cru skills list` | | List discovered agent skills | 186 | `cru tasks list` | | Manage tasks from TASKS.md | 187 | `cru daemon start` | | Start background daemon | 188 | `cru daemon status` | | Check daemon status | 189 | `cru storage verify` | | Verify content integrity | 190 | `cru auth login` | | Store LLM provider API key | 191 192 Run `cru --help` for full options. 193 194 ## Roadmap 195 196 - [x] TUI chat with session persistence and resume 197 - [x] MCP server for external agents 198 - [x] Lua/Fennel plugin system (17+ API modules) 199 - [x] Block-level semantic search with reranking 200 - [x] Precognition (auto-RAG before each turn) 201 - [x] Daemon with auto-spawn, file watching, multi-session support 202 - [ ] Web chat interface 203 - [x] ACP host mode (use Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode through Crucible) 204 - [ ] ACP agent mode (embed Crucible in editors like Zed, JetBrains, Neovim) 205 206 ## License 207 208 MIT or Apache-2.0, at your option. [208 lines read, 208 total]"}

data.description: Read file contents. Returns content with line numbers.

result.result:
{"result":"     1	# AI Agent Guide for Crucible
     2	
     3	> Instructions for AI agents (Claude, Codex, etc.) working on the Crucible codebase
     4	
     5	## Project Overview
     6	
     7	**Crucible** is a knowledge-grounded agent runtime. Agents that draw from a knowledge graph make better decisions вҖ” memory and knowledge are too fundamental to be an afterthought. Your notes, sessions, and wikilinks form that graph. Everything beyond the knowledge core is extensible.
     8	
     9	**Core Pillars:**
    10	- **Knowledge + Agents** вҖ” agents draw from and contribute to a knowledge graph; Precognition injects relevant context before every turn
    11	- **PKM as input** вҖ” notes, wikilinks, tags, and sessions-as-notes are how knowledge enters the system
    12	- **Neovim-like architecture** вҖ” Lua extensibility, TUI-first, headless daemon with RPC, plugin-driven
    13	- **Plaintext-first** вҖ” markdown files are source of truth; swap LLM backends freely
    14	
    15	## Architecture
    16	
    17	### Crate Organization
    18	
    19	| Crate | Purpose | Key Types |
    20	|-------|---------|-----------|
    21	| `crucible-core` | Domain logic, traits, parser types; includes parser module (absorbed from crucible-parser) | `Provider`, `CanEmbed`, `CanChat`, `ParsedNote` |
    22	| `crucible-cli` | Terminal UI, REPL, commands | `InkChatApp`, `ChatAppMsg` |
    23	| `crucible-oil` | Terminal rendering primitives | `Node`, `render_to_string` |
    24	| `crucible-web` | Browser chat UI (SolidJS + Axum) | HTTP/SSE endpoints |
    25	| `crucible-sqlite` | SQLite storage (default); fast, lightweight; includes query/ module | `SqliteStorage` |
    26	| `crucible-lua` | Lua/Luau with Fennel support | `LuaExecutor`, `FennelCompiler` |
    27	| `crucible-llm` | Embedding backends (FastEmbed, Ollama, OpenAI) | `EmbeddingProvider` |
    28	| `crucible-config` | Configuration types and loading | `AppConfig`, provider configs |
    29	| `crucible-acp` | Agent Context Protocol | Protocol types |
    30	| `crucible-daemon` | Daemon server (library); includes enrichment pipeline, note processing, RPC client, observability, file watching, skills discovery, and tools (absorbed from crucible-tools) | `Server`, `SessionManager`, `AgentManager` |
    31	| `crucible-lance` | LanceDB vector storage backend | `LanceStore`, `LanceNoteStore` |
    32	
    33	### Terminology: Kiln vs Workspace vs Project
    34	
    35	These three terms have precise meanings in Crucible вҖ” do not use them interchangeably:
    36	
    37	| Term | Definition | Config File |
    38	|------|------------|-------------|
    39	| **Project** | Where work output is done/put. A registered directory in the daemon (git repo root or invocation directory). Contains code, configs, build artifacts. | `.crucible/project.toml` |
    40	| **Kiln** | Where accrued knowledge goes. A content directory for notes, sessions, and linked knowledge. | `.crucible/kiln.toml` |
    41	| **Workspace** | A specific instance of a project directory. Often the same dir as project root, could be a worktree. **Runtime concept вҖ” no config file.** | вҖ” |
    42	
    43	**Correct uses of "workspace"** (do NOT rename these):
    44	- `session.workspace` вҖ” the working directory for a session
    45	- `WorkspaceTools` вҖ” tools scoped to the working directory
    46	- `workspace: &Path` parameters in agent code
    47	- Lua `paths.workspace()` вҖ” returns the working directory
    48	
    49	**Config files** (`.crucible/` directory inside a kiln or project root):
    50	- `kiln.toml` вҖ” kiln identity: name, data classification
    51	- `project.toml` вҖ” project config: attached kilns, security policies
    52	- Old `workspace.toml` is still read for backward compatibility (read-only fallback)
    53	
    54	### Daemon Architecture
    55	
    56	Crucible uses a **single `cru` binary** with a built-in daemon for multi-session support:
    57	
    58	```
    59	в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    60	в”Ӯ  CLI (cru)                    Daemon (cru daemon serve)     в”Ӯ
    61	в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ              в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
    62	в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ cru chat    в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Unix Socket Server       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
    63	в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ cru search  в”Ӯ   JSON-RPC   в”Ӯ ($XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
    64	в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ cru process в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ  crucible.sock)          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
    65	в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ              в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
    66	в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ Managers:                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
    67	в”Ӯ  All storage via daemon RPC   в”Ӯ вҖў KilnManager            в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
    68	в”Ӯ  вҶ’ DaemonClient.connect()     в”Ӯ вҖў SessionManager         в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
    69	в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ вҖў AgentManager           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
    70	в”Ӯ  Auto-spawn: cru daemon serve в”Ӯ вҖў SubscriptionManager    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
    71	в”Ӯ  (forks self as daemon)       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
    72	в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    73	```
    74	- **Socket path**: `$CRUCIBLE_SOCKET` env var, or `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/crucible.sock`, or `/tmp/crucible.sock`
    75	- **Storage**: Daemon-only (SQLite + LanceDB). CLI has zero direct storage access.
    76	- **Auto-spawn**: `DaemonClient::connect_or_start()` forks `cru daemon serve` if not running
    77	- **Protocol**: JSON-RPC 2.0 over Unix socket with async event streaming
    78	
    79	**RPC methods:**
    80	- Kiln: `kiln.open`, `kiln.close`, `kiln.list`, `search_vectors`, `list_notes`, `get_note_by_name`
    81	- Sessions: `session.create`, `session.list`, `session.get`, `session.load`, `session.pause`, `session.resume`, `session.end`
    82	- Agents: `session.configure_agent`, `session.send_message`, `session.cancel`, `session.switch_model`, `session.list_models`
    83	- Config: `session.set_thinking_budget`, `session.get_thinking_budget`
    84	- Events: `session.subscribe`, `session.unsubscribe`
    85	
    86	### Cross-Layer Feature Checklist
    87	
    88	When implementing features that affect agent/session behavior (not just UI display):
    89	
    90	**Scope Classification:**
    91	| Scope | Examples | Where State Lives |
    92	|-------|----------|-------------------|
    93	| Session-scoped | model, thinking_budget, temperature | Daemon `SessionAgent`, synced via RPC |
    94	| TUI-local | theme, show_thinking, verbose | `InkChatApp` fields, no RPC needed |
    95	
    96	**Before Implementing:**
    97	- [ ] Check if daemon already has RPC for this (`crucible-daemon/src/rpc_client/`)
    98	- [ ] Check if `SessionAgent` has a field for this (`crucible-core/src/session/types.rs`)
    99	- [ ] Determine scope: Does this need multi-client consistency? If yes вҶ’ session-scoped
   100	

[100 lines read, 467 total]"}

data.content: Now I have a good understanding of the project. Let me provide a comprehensive summary.

data.full_response:
I'll explore the repository to understand what it contains.## Crucible

**Crucible** is a **knowledge-grounded agent runtime** вҖ” a local-first system where AI agents draw from and contribute to a personal knowledge graph built from your notes, conversations, and wikilinks.

### Key Concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Kiln** | A content directory storing notes, sessions, and knowledge (plaintext markdown) |
| **Precognition** | Auto-injects relevant context from your knowledge graph before each LLM turn |
| **Sessions as Notes** | Every chat saves as markdown вҖ” searchable, linkable, versionable in git |
| **MCP Server** | Exposes knowledge tools to Claude/GPT/local models |
| **ACP** | Agent Context Protocol вҖ” spawn Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, etc. through Crucible |

### Architecture

It's a **Rust workspace** with 11 crates:

| Crate | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `crucible-core` | Domain logic, traits, parser types |
| `crucible-cli` | Terminal UI, REPL, commands |
| `crucible-daemon` | Background daemon with RPC (Unix socket), session management, file watching |
| `crucible-llm` | LLM/embedding backends (Ollama, OpenAI, FastEmbed) |
| `crucible-sqlite` | SQLite storage |
| `crucible-lance` | LanceDB vector storage |
| `crucible-lua` | Lua/Fennel plugin system |
| `crucible-web` | Browser chat UI (SolidJS + Axum) |
| `crucible-acp` | Agent Context Protocol support |
| `crucible-oil` | Terminal rendering primitives |

### Notable Features

- **Neovim-like**: TUI-first, headless daemon with RPC, Lua/Fennel plugins
- **Plaintext-first**: Markdown files are the source of truth; no proprietary formats
- **Block-level semantic search**: Embeddings at paragraph granularity
- **Multi-agent orchestration**: Agents can delegate to each other via ACP

### Tech Stack

- **Rust 1.75+** with Tokio async runtime
- **Storage**: SQLite + LanceDB (vector embeddings)
- **Protocol**: JSON-RPC 2.0 over Unix socket
- **Frontend**: Ratatmoi (TUI), SolidJS (web)
- **Plugins**: Lua 5.4 via mlua

### Project Status

Version **0.4.0**, early development. Active with CI, documentation site, and recent commits. Licensed MIT OR Apache-2.0.
